events.title: Venezuela quake death toll tops 5,000 as IMF releases recovery funds
events.description: More than 5,000 people were killed in the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month, officials said, adding that the country would tap $346 million from the International Monetary Fund (IMF) for reconstruction. The coastal state of La Guaira, located north of Caracas, was decimated in the quakes that struck within a minute of each other on June 24.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela quake death toll tops 5,000 as IMF releases recovery funds

events.title: Magnitude 7.3 quake hits Mexican coast near Guatemala, tsunami alert lifted
events.description: A powerful earthquake struck the southern Mexican Pacific coast on Friday, prompting residents to evacuate buildings in neighbouring Guatemala and El Salvador – and triggering an alert from the US Tsunami Warning System that was later lifted. There were no immediate reports of damage or casualties.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Magnitude 7.3 quake hits Mexican coast near Guatemala, tsunami alert lifted

events.title: France: 2,000 hectares lost in forest fires
events.description: France has been battling a series of devastating wildfires following record-breaking heatwaves, with the government saying the country has not experienced so many fire outbreaks since World War II. One of the largest blazes has scorched thousands of hectares in the historic Fontainebleau forest, south of Paris. In response, the Fondation du Patrimoine has launched a fundraising campaign to help restore the damaged woodland by replanting the forest. Elitsa Gadeva reports.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France: 2,000 hectares lost in forest fires

events.title: ‘It’s only going to get worse’: wildfires forcing firefighters to make impossible choices
events.description: As the climate crisis fuels more intense blazes, pushing them to new parts of the world, those tackling them are forced to ration resources and decide which to fight César Alcaraz had only just become a firefighter in the late 1990s when he found himself ambushed by a fast-moving blaze. Barely able to breathe and with no more water left in his truck, he and his colleagues fled an inferno ravaging Spain’s Montgó mountain region, wishing their bosses had sent more support. But nearly three decades on, as an officer with Alicante’s provincial firefighters, Alcaraz has more sympathy for the agonising choices that commanders have to make. When wildfires overwhelm an area, his job resembles that of a doctor in an emergency room with too few ventilators. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘It’s only going to get worse’: wildfires forcing firefighters to make impossible choices

events.title: Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads
events.description: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoors Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Tell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada? Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canada and parts of Minnesota is spreading across the US, prompting air quality alerts in more than 20 states with millions of Americans expected to face unhealthy air conditions this week. The smoke from the more than 180 active wildfires in northern Ontario made Chicago’s air quality the worst in the world on Thursday evening, followed by Detroit and Minneapolis, according to IQAir’s global rankings. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads

events.title: Magnitude 7.3 earthquake quake strikes off Mexico coast
events.description: The US Tsunami Warning Center has recorded waves 0.3m (1.1 feet) high in Puerto Madero and Chiapas in Mexico after the quake.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Magnitude 7.3 earthquake quake strikes off Mexico coast

events.title: Germany news: Minister warns of high terror threat
events.description: Germany's interior minister has warned of a heightened security threat, saying an attack could be imminent. Meanwhile, a train security guard was critically injured after falling from a moving train. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany news: Minister warns of high terror threat

events.title: Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike
events.description: More than 256,000 people in Djibouti – around 25 per cent of the population – are likely to face crisis or emergency level hunger over the coming months, a UN-backed global food monitoring network warned in a new report released Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Djibouti: Emergency alert spotlights sharp hunger spike

events.title: Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake
events.description: Thousands of people remain displaced three weeks after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela and the possible formation of a strong El Niño climate pattern could worsen their already dire situation, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake

events.title: Gaza officials say several killed in Israeli strike
events.description: Palestinian health and civil defense officials said at least eight people were killed while attending a funeral. Israel's military said it had struck a "terrorist cell" from the Islamic Jihad militant group.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza officials say several killed in Israeli strike

events.title: Donald Trump blames Canada for smoke pollution
events.description: More than 800 wildfires are burning across Canada, including around 190 in northern Ontario, with the surge in fires widely linked to climate change. Thick smoke has created hazardous air quality not only across parts of Canada but also in several US states, including Minnesota, Illinois, Michigan and New York. The pollution has raised concerns ahead of Sunday's World Cup final at the open-air stadium in New Jersey. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has blamed Canada for its forest management and threatened additional tariffs.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Donald Trump blames Canada for smoke pollution

events.title: Russia says Ukrainian drone attack killed several in Moscow logistics centre
events.description: A local official said that a Ukrainian drone attack killed seven night-shift workers and wounded 24 at a Moscow logistics centre on Saturday, as part of a larger-scale attack including 370 drones, most of which were "neutralised" according to the city mayor. Kyiv says its attacks on Russia are fair retribution for Moscow's four-year war in Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia says Ukrainian drone attack killed several in Moscow logistics centre

events.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
events.description: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

events.title: CDC and FDA link lettuce at Taco Bell in five states to cyclospora outbreak
events.description: FDA investigation identified a single supplier of the lettuce, but federal warnings did not name the company Federal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served by Taco Bell locations across five US states as a source of the widespread outbreak of the diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora . The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) confirmed the source late on Thursday and warned consumers not to eat shredded iceberg lettuce from Taco Bell restaurants in Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 CDC and FDA link lettuce at Taco Bell in five states to cyclospora outbreak

events.title: ‘Malvinas are Argentinian’: World Cup holders celebrate win over England with Falklands banner
events.description: Banner refers to Falkland Islands conflict in 1982 UK business secretary calls for Fifa investigation The Argentina players celebrated their World Cup win over England with a banner saying “ Las Malvinas son Argentinas ”, making reference to the 1982 Falklands war . Argentina were 1-0 down with five minutes to go of the semi-final in Atlanta but rallied and scored twice in quick succession to reach a second straight World Cup final , where they will face Spain in New Jersey on Sunday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Malvinas are Argentinian’: World Cup holders celebrate win over England with Falklands banner

events.title: Canada wildfires: Smoke choking major US cities
events.description: Trump has blamed Canada for the wildfires and their impact on the US, threatening extra tariffs on the US neighbor. FIFA World Cup organizers are "monitoring closely" the smoky conditions.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Canada wildfires: Smoke choking major US cities

events.title: Iran threatens 'full-scale' offensive if US keeps bombing
events.description: An Iranian general warned no border will be safe if the US strikes continue past the next few days. Meanwhile, the US rejected Tehran's claims that two oil tankers exploded near the Strait of Hormuz. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens 'full-scale' offensive if US keeps bombing

events.title: Thousands protest in Kyiv for second day over ouster of popular defence minister Fedorov
events.description: Protesters rallied for a second day in the Ukrainian capital on Friday, angered by President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision to fire youthful defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen as an innovator who pioneered the country’s successful drone warfare but clashed with the traditional military establishment.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Thousands protest in Kyiv for second day over ouster of popular defence minister Fedorov

events.title: Iran-backed terror proxy Houthis threaten fresh attacks after Yemen airport strike
events.description: The Houthis threatened to strike King Khalid Airport in Riyadh after what they called "blatant aggression" targeting the Sanaa airport runway.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-backed terror proxy Houthis threaten fresh attacks after Yemen airport strike

events.title: Trump administration probes UNRWA over alleged employment of 1,500 terrorists
events.description: A senior U.S. official says the USAID OIG investigation picked up where the U.N. failed, examining UNRWA staff links to terrorism beyond Oct. 7.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump administration probes UNRWA over alleged employment of 1,500 terrorists

events.title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war

events.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
events.description: On Wednesday, the Security Council heard an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing was held as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur

events.title: Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US, potential risk for World Cup final
events.description: Plumes of smoke from wildfires in Canada are pouring into major US cities.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US, potential risk for World Cup final

events.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
events.description: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organization before Uganda can officially be declared Ebola-free, provided no new infections are detected. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump blames Canada as US 'invaded by filthy air'
events.description: US President Donald Trump has blamed Canada for wildfire smoke that could impact the World Cup final.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump blames Canada as US 'invaded by filthy air'

events.title: US-Iran war: UN chief calls for new diplomatic push following attacks on civilian areas
events.description: The UN Secretary-General remains deeply concerned by the continuing deadly military escalation between Iran and the United States, his spokesperson said on Friday as attacks destroyed key civilian and military infrastructure across the Gulf region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran war: UN chief calls for new diplomatic push following attacks on civilian areas

events.title: World News in Brief: Appeal for calm in Kashmir, women on the move, increased Ebola deaths in communities, 12th anniversary of MH17
events.description: The UN human rights chief, Volker Türk, has appealed for calm in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir amid unrest ahead of regional elections.

events.title: More Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US midwest, mid-Atlantic and north-east
events.description: 109 million people face another day of poor air quality as smoke from blazes in Ontario drifts over the US Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Tens of millions of Americans are enduring another day of smoky skies , irritated eyes and bad air quality, as Canadian wildfire smoke spread again over huge swathes of the US, affecting about 109 million people across the midwest, mid-Atlantic and north-east. The pungent smoke blanketed cities such as Chicago and Detroit, where residents on Friday were warned to stay indoors and reduce activity levels after the air-quality index reached a “hazardous” 361, according to the government website AirNow . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ More Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US midwest, mid-Atlantic and north-east

events.title: Amid elections, Sao Tome and Principe balances global powers
events.description: As voters head to the polls, Sao Tome and Principe is attracting international scrutiny. A military agreement with Russia has heightened interest in the strategically located archipelago.

events.title: Paris region's record June heatwave more than doubled normal mortality rate to reach 3,000
events.description: Authorities in the Paris region recorded "1,565 more deaths than expected" over a one-week period in June as the country grappled with a protracted heatwave that broke all-time temperature records, France's public health agency said in a report released on Friday, adding that people over 65 accounted for more than 80 percent of fatalities.

events.title: Florida wildlife officials confirm 13-foot gator killed Orlando woman with 'death roll' on river
events.description: FWC trappers captured two massive alligators after the Econlockhatchee River attack, and lab results now link one to Brittany Clark's death.

events.title: Rubio convenes countries on left-wing political violence
events.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Rubio convenes countries on left-wing political violence

events.title: Gaza farmers battle to rebuild as WFP warns of West Bank settler violence
events.description: The UN is supporting farmers in Gaza who are struggling to rebuild their smallholdings in areas devastated by the Hamas-Israel war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza farmers battle to rebuild as WFP warns of West Bank settler violence

events.title: At least 14 dead after minibus rolls off mountain road in Peru
events.description: At least 14 people have been killed and five injured after a minibus in the Peruvian Andes lost control on a curve.

events.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
events.description: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers with large balls of hail. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave

events.title: WHO warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak is outpacing response as Uganda reaches milestone
events.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has become the third largest on record and is spreading faster than any previous one during the first month of exposure, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO warns DR Congo Ebola outbreak is outpacing response as Uganda reaches milestone

events.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
events.description: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities

events.title: Peter Falconio murder: British expert says he has identified a ‘most likely’ burial location
events.description: UK police adviser in the early 2000s and global consultant in ‘no-body’ homicide cases says he has narrowed down the outback search area • Peter Falconio murder 25 years on: new footage shows dying Australian outback killer’s refusal to reveal body’s location The former British government expert who consulted on the search for the remains of the murdered backpacker Peter Falconio says he has now identified a “most likely” potential burial location – an abandoned racetrack only 8km from the scene of the infamous outback attack at Barrow Creek. In July 2001, Falconio and his partner, Joanne Lees, both from Yorkshire, were ambushed and attacked by Bradley John Murdoch as they drove along a remote stretch of road in Australia’s Northern Territory, about 300km north of Alice Springs. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Peter Falconio murder: British expert says he has identified a ‘most likely’ burial location

events.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
events.description: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins

events.title: More than 100 homes destroyed as firefighters battle Norway blaze
events.description: More than 100 homes have been ‌destroyed in a major fire in southern Norway, forcing the evacuation of hundreds of people, rescue officials ​have said.

events.title: New guidance offers hope for reducing the global toll of dementia
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has issued updated global guidelines aimed at reducing the risk of cognitive decline and dementia, saying that up to 45 per cent of cases may be preventable or delayed by addressing modifiable risk factors throughout life.

events.title: Shooting near ICE detention facility in Aurora, Colorado, leaves 1 injured, person detained
events.description: A woman suffered injuries that were not life-threatening in a shooting near an Aurora, Colorado, ICE facility as one person was detained for questioning.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Shooting near ICE detention facility in Aurora, Colorado, leaves 1 injured, person detained

events.title: Iranian official says the policy of negotiating during war is over

events.title: Cuban dissident artist Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara cleared to travel to US
events.description: Whereabouts of artist imprisoned after 2021 protests had been unknown following end of his prison term in July Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, a prominent Cuban dissident artist whose whereabouts remained unknown after his prison sentence ended last week, has been given permission to travel to the US, according to his official Facebook page. Otero Alcántara, designated a prisoner of conscience by Amnesty International, was sentenced to five years in prison in 2022 for insulting national symbols, contempt and disturbing the public order. Continue reading...

events.title: Baltics and Poland warn Russia could launch limited military or hybrid provocation against NATO

events.title: ‘Heat deaths are not inevitable’: WHO pushes Europe to heat‑proof its hospitals
events.description: As Europe battles a deadly record-breaking heatwave, the World Health Organization (WHO) issued new guidance on Thursday to help countries face scorching temperatures now and in the future.

events.title: Inside Israel's mission to train civilians to stop the next Oct 7-like terror attack
events.description: A full-scale terror attack drill at Kibbutz Bror Hayil simulates Oct. 7-style infiltration, training civil defense squads near the Gaza border.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Inside Israel's mission to train civilians to stop the next Oct 7-like terror attack

events.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
events.description: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles. Continue reading...

events.title: Robodebt whistleblower was told her royal commission evidence ‘could cost you your job’, court hears
events.description: Exclusive: Jeannie-Marie Blake is suing the Australian government over alleged threats, which her department denies making Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A key robodebt whistleblower is suing the Australian government, alleging she was threatened before she appeared at a royal commission and was warned that her testimony “could cost you your job”. Services Australia whistleblower Jeannie-Marie Blake has filed proceedings in the federal court, alleging her department made repeated threats against her before and after her explosive evidence to the robodebt royal commission. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Robodebt whistleblower was told her royal commission evidence ‘could cost you your job’, court hears

events.title: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
events.description: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.

events.title: Why the US is going after the International Criminal Court
events.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has ramped up his criticism of the International Criminal Court in The Hague. His gloves-off approach could have far-reaching consequences for the tribunal.

events.title: At least 20 children and 1 adult were killed in a school bus crash in Uganda
events.description: At least 20 children and one adult were killed when an elementary school bus crashed in the East African country of Uganda

events.title: Iran calls on Houthis to prepare to cut off Red Sea gateway — can the terror group do it?
events.description: Iran reportedly told Houthis to prepare to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait with missiles, drones and sea mines if the U.S. strikes Iranian infrastructure.

events.title: Uganda suspends school trips after 20 pupils die in bus crash
events.description: Thousands are killed on Uganda's roads each year but this is one of its deadliest accidents involving children.

events.title: Taco Bell lettuce tied to diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak
events.description: US health officials linked iceberg lettuce served at Taco Bell to an outbreak of the parasite cyclospora, which causes “explosive” diarrhea. Health officials warn that the contamination may go beyond the fast-food chain.

events.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh night
events.description: Kuwait said on Saturday that an Iranian attack hit a water and electricity plant one day after a similar strike damaged another plant. Tehran launched attacks on US military sites hosted by several of its Gulf neighbours in response to a seventh night of US strikes on Iran overnight on Friday. Morgan Ayre reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strikes hit Iran for seventh night

events.title: Europe's early heat wave led to a spike in deaths, may have killed over 10,000
events.description: Heat that hit Europe exceptionally early and hard this year appears to have led to a spike in deaths, with well over 10,000 more people dying at the height of the heat wave than would normally have been expected, according to figures that are still eme...

events.title: College student's fatal canoe trip takes turn after lone survivor is arrested months later
events.description: Michael Collins, 20, allegedly operated a canoe while drunk before it capsized on Lake Hayward, killing Dominick Tocci in a March drowning.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 College student's fatal canoe trip takes turn after lone survivor is arrested months later

events.title: Trump won’t rule out Kharg Island takeover: What a US assault could look like
events.description: Military experts detail how U.S. Marines could seize Iran's Kharg Island in hours but warn holding it against missile and drone attacks is far riskier.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump won’t rule out Kharg Island takeover: What a US assault could look like

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: West Indies cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies
events.description: Sir Garfield Sobers, considered one of the game's greatest all-rounders, has died aged 89. The International Cricket Council said he was one of the "greatest cricketers to have ever graced the game."
events.ticker_label: 💥 West Indies cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies

events.title: UN launches new refugee hub in Ethiopia offering legal aid and protection
events.description: In tonight's edition, Algeria mourns the 11 people killed in a deadly orphanage fire as investigators search for the cause. Ethiopia and the UN refugee agency launch a new support centre offering refugees free legal advice. Meanwhile, Drill music is booming in Ethiopia, winning over young fans while fuelling debate over its place in a conservative society.

events.title: Ukraine: Trouble at the Top? Adieu Keir, Alright Andy
events.description: In a week that’s seen rare wartime demonstrations in Ukraine protesting the ousting of Defence Minister Mykhailo (Misha) Fedorov after six months in a job in which he’s been credited with making a difference on the battlefield and in an anti-corruption drive. But he had reportedly clashed with Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, over how the war should be waged.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Trouble at the Top? Adieu Keir, Alright Andy

events.title: Ukrainian prime minister resigns in Zelenskyy shake-up
events.description: Yulia Svyrydenko resigned as Ukraine's prime minister, with Zelenskyy saying he offered her a new role in relations with a key international partner.

events.title: A seventh night of US strikes cuts water to villages in Iran’s south

events.title: Middle East live: Kuwait says Iran hit second water and electricity plant
events.description: Kuwait said on Saturday that an Iranian attack hit a water and electricity plant one day after a similar strike damaged another plant. Tehran launched attacks on US military sites hosted by several of its Gulf neighbours in response to a seventh night of US strikes on Iran overnight on Friday. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

events.title: 'Inclusive' World Cup fell short as Trump administration shut out fans, rights groups say
events.description: FIFA failed to deliver on a pledge to make the 2026 World Cup the "most inclusive" in history as the Trump administration's visa policies shut out fans from several countries and even barred a referee from officiating at the tournament, the Sport & Rights Alliance, a coalition of rights groups, said on Thursday, adding that it would release a report in September detailing human rights issues at the tournament.

events.title: Russia is the only winner from Ukraine's internal war
events.description: An extraordinary rupture at the heart of Ukraine's top war-fighting team is a dangerous moment for the country that can only benefit Russia.

events.title: 7.3 earthquake hits Mexico-Guatemala border with no immediate damage reported
events.description: A strong earthquake has struck the southern Mexican Pacific coast, right on the border with Guatemala
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 7.3 earthquake hits Mexico-Guatemala border with no immediate damage reported

events.title: UK pins string of antisemitic attacks on Iran-linked group, bans IRGC
events.description: Keir Starmer announced sweeping national security powers after British intelligence linked antisemitic arson attacks to an Iran-backed proxy.

events.title: Four Americans caught in horrific Mexico highway pileup that killed at least 10
events.description: A fiery chain-reaction crash involving multiple tractor-trailers killed at least 10 people in western Mexico, while four Americans were transported to local hospitals.

events.title: Air conditioner sparked deadly blaze at children's orphanage - police
events.description: Eleven people, including children, died in the fire that also injured 19 others.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Air conditioner sparked deadly blaze at children's orphanage - police

events.title: Olympic cyclist admits to driving despite ban after crash that killed wife
events.description: Rohan Dennis pleaded guilty to driving while disqualified after police pulled him over last month.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Olympic cyclist admits to driving despite ban after crash that killed wife

events.title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
events.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull. Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife wasn’t a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu. Continue reading...

events.title: U.S. strikes bridges in Iran; Tehran targets U.S. bases in the Gulf
events.description: The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. strikes bridges in Iran; Tehran targets U.S. bases in the Gulf

events.title: How conflict in Congo, Sudan and Yemen is fueling the spread of disease
events.description: Whether fast-spreading cholera in Sudan and Yemen or the Ebola virus in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), outbreaks of disease tend to be especially deadly when they take hold amid armed conflict.

events.title: Ukraine: Protests over Fedorov's removal continue for a second day
events.description: Protesters in Kyiv have demanded the dismissal of Ukraine's commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, as public anger continues to grow following President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's surprise cabinet reshuffle. The demonstrations entered a second day after the popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, was not reappointed. The 35-year-old has been widely credited with contributing to Ukraine's recent successes in its war with Russia, making his removal a source of significant public backlash.

events.title: Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software
events.description: Whistleblower suggests internal security services deployed spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targets A former member of Morocco’s domestic intelligence service has helped to provide an unprecedented insight into how the north African state used hacking software – including Pegasus spyware – to target journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers. Pegasus, which is manufactured by the Israel-based NSO Group, allows its operator to access everything on a target’s mobile phone, including emails, text messages and photographs. It can also activate the phone’s recorder and camera, turning it into a listening device. Continue reading...

events.title: NSW woman arrested over movements of Dezi Freeman – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has been called “un-Australian” over her appearance on a podcast with far-right UK activist, Tommy Robinson. The Greens senator Sarah Hanson-Young told ABC RN Hanson must return to Australia and apologise. This is so appalling, and frankly, it’s unAustralian. To go overseas to hang out with a criminal thug … to be laughing on his show about multiculturalism back here in Australia – which are our communities, Australian citizens, and the people who make this country great, Pauline Hanson is the most unAustralian politician in the parliament, and she should come home, face the music, and apologise. I‘m not sure where Ms Hanson is getting her figures from, but they’ve never been provided to me as the minister for disability and the minister for the NDIS. I suspect they don’t exist. I’m loath to respond to a podcast between Ms Hanson and this convicted criminal, who frankly has been disowned by so many leading figures on the right. Continue reading...

events.title: India news: CJP to call for Modi's resignation after Sonam Wangchuk 'forcefully' shifted to hospital
events.description: Abhijeet Dipke said the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will now demand the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi after Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly shifted to a hospital by police. DW has more.

events.title: 'Shameless' fraudster who stole £300k from vulnerable pensioner led away by police
events.description: This is the moment a "shameless" fraudster who stole £300,000 from a vulnerable pensioner is led away by police, after Sky News tracked her down to Tenerife.

events.title: Home Alone 2 actress has died
events.description: Brenda Fricker - the first Irish woman to win an Oscar - has died aged 81, her agent said.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Home Alone 2 actress has died

events.title: Trump accused of making 'totally bogus' election claims in primetime address to nation
events.description: Donald Trump has been accused of using an address to the nation to make "totally bogus" claims of interference in the 2020 US election.

events.title: A Brazilian bay’s surprise shark nursery sparks conservation project
events.description: Scientists have discovered that a cove in Brazil's Ilha Grande bay is a nursery for pregnant blacktip sharks

events.title: Japan's parliament enshrines male-only succession for the shrinking imperial family
events.description: Japan’s parliament has enacted a historic revision to the 19th-century Imperial House Law by insisting only paternal-lineage men can become emperor

events.title: Andy Burnham is poised to become Britain's next prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham is about to become Britain’s 59th prime minister, following the sudden downfall of Labour Prime Minister Keir Starmer

events.title: Andy Burnham is declared leader of UK's Labour Party, pledges to restore hope
events.description: Andy Burnham has been officially declared leader of Britain’s governing Labour Party, clearing his final hurdle to taking office as prime minister next week

events.title: Why Britain is getting a new prime minister without a general election
events.description: Veteran Labour Party politician Andy Burnham is set to become Britain’s next prime minister on Monday

events.title: Danish police officer and 2 others shot, including suspected perpetrator
events.description: Danish police say that a police officer and two other people have been shot including the suspected perpetrator

events.title: Israel's parliament dissolves ahead of Oct. 27 elections
events.description: Israel's parliament has dissolved after passing a series of controversial bills in marathon sessions in its last few days

events.title: China rejects Trump's election interference claim as 'groundless accusations'
events.description: China says it has never interfered in U.S. elections and has no interest in doing so

events.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

events.title: Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health
events.description: For Tanatswa Amanda Chikaura, mental health advocacy began with a personal loss: while studying psychology at the University of Zimbabwe, she learned that a fellow student had died by suicide.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Breaking the silence: Zimbabwean youth leader calls for urgent investment in mental health

events.title: Alabama mom charged with murder after toddler bound, left to suffocate for 12 hours: cops
events.description: Sierra Hadaway faces murder charges after 2-year-old Madilynn was found bound and face-down on a bed for over 12 hours in Lanett, Alabama.

events.title: 'America's Most Wanted' fugitive captured on sailboat off NJ coast after 20 years on the run
events.description: The former anesthesiologist featured on "America's Most Wanted" was living as "Richard Graydon" aboard a sailboat named The Silver Lining for decades.

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Intro to biology, dancing on graves and AI backlash
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: Video shows Canadian illegal alien slapping teen over Trump, ICE clothing: DHS
events.description: DHS shared video of the Point Pleasant Beach boardwalk incident as the Canadian visa overstay suspect faces removal proceedings while in ICE custody.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: DHS seeks custody of illegal immigrant charged with murder after Trump deportation, Biden release
events.description: ICE has filed a detainer against Akbor Miah, a Bangladeshi illegal immigrant charged with murdering his sister-in-law in Lake County, Florida.

events.title: Asian nation with 1,500-year-old imperial line insists only men can become emperor in policy revision
events.description: Japan's Parliament revised the Imperial House Law to cement male-only succession, blocking Princess Aiko from inheriting the throne despite public support.

events.title: Resource-rich nation praises US ties amid Washington-Beijing critical minerals race
events.description: Foreign Minister Thérèse Kayikwamba Wagner says the Democratic Republic of the Congo needs multiple partners to develop its cobalt, copper and lithium wealth amid growing U.S. interest.

events.title: African growth boom follows Trump push to replace aid with trade
events.description: Despite predictions of collapse after USAID cuts, sub-Saharan Africa is outpacing Asia in growth forecasts, fueled by trade and private investment.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham? The Trump critic set to become the UK's next prime minister
events.description: Andy Burnham secured 322 MP nominations on day one of the leadership process, but critics say he faces less scrutiny than any recent U.K. prime minister.

events.title: Trump officials unveil private sector blueprint for life after USAID
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says the Trump administration is replacing traditional foreign aid with private investment and trade to create jobs, cut dependency.

events.title: FBI snares an American heir indicted for allegedly bankrolling anti-cop, pro-Hamas communist revolution
events.description: James Cox Chambers Jr., estranged heir to the $27 billion Cox empire, was detained in Spain for alleged money laundering and Hamas support.
